patches.hypothesis: The code does not consider the `JsonFormat.Shape.ANY` case to determine the shape based on the presence of a pattern or format feature, which defaults to string representation.
patches.changes: + if (shape == JsonFormat.Shape.ANY && (format.hasPattern() || format.hasLocale() || format.hasTimeZone())) { + TimeZone tz = format.getTimeZone(); + final String pattern = format.hasPattern() ? format.getPattern() : StdDateFormat.DATE_FORMAT_STR_ISO8601; + final Locale loc = format.hasLocale() ? format.getLocale() : serializers.getLocale(); + SimpleDateFormat df = new SimpleDateFormat(pattern, loc); + if (tz == null) { + tz = serializers.getTimeZone(); + } + df.setTimeZone(tz); + return withFormat(Boolean.FALSE, df); + }
patches.diff: + if (shape == JsonFormat.Shape.ANY && (format.hasPattern() || format.hasLocale() || format.hasTimeZone())) { + TimeZone tz = format.getTimeZone(); + final String pattern = format.hasPattern() ? format.getPattern() : StdDateFormat.DATE_FORMAT_STR_ISO8601; + final Locale loc = format.hasLocale() ? format.getLocale() : serializers.getLocale(); + SimpleDateFormat df = new SimpleDateFormat(pattern, loc); + if (tz == null) { + tz = serializers.getTimeZone(); + } + df.setTimeZone(tz); + return withFormat(Boolean.FALSE, df); + }

patches.hypothesis: The test case indicates that the serialization format should be adjusted based on the presence of a pattern or a locale, even if the shape is not explicitly JSONFormat.Shape.STRING. The method currently does not handle these cases properly.
patches.changes:
- Change handling of JsonFormat.Shape to interpret ANY as STRING when a pattern is specified or when only locale/timezone is specified
+ Adjust the condition to serialize as STRING when pattern or locale is specified
patches.diff:
- Change handling of JsonFormat.Shape to interpret ANY as STRING when a pattern is specified or when only locale/timezone is specified
+ Adjust the condition to serialize as STRING when pattern or locale is specified
